title: Marketing Analytics Dashboard
description: Build a marketing KPI dashboard using Google Analytics and Google Sheets (or Looker Studio) that tracks the metrics that matter. Learn to turn raw data into actionable insights.
whyItMatters: Data-driven decision making separates good marketers from great ones. A well-built dashboard gives you and your stakeholders real-time visibility into what's working and what needs attention.

steps.title: Define dashboard purpose
steps.description: Who is this dashboard for? (Marketing team, CEO, client?) What decisions should it help them make? This determines which metrics to include.

steps.title: Select KPIs
steps.description: Choose 8-12 KPIs across the funnel: awareness (impressions, reach), engagement (CTR, session duration), conversion (conversion rate, leads), and revenue (ROAS, CLV, CAC).
steps.tips: Less is more. A dashboard with 50 metrics helps no one. Pick the ones that drive decisions.

steps.title: Set up data sources
steps.description: Connect Google Analytics 4 (use the demo account if needed). Set up Google Search Console. Export social media and email data.

steps.title: Build the dashboard
steps.description: Use Google Sheets or Looker Studio. Create sections: overview (headline metrics), acquisition (traffic sources), engagement (content performance), and conversion (goal completions). Add charts and trendlines.

steps.title: Add context and targets
steps.description: Raw numbers mean nothing without context. Add: period-over-period comparisons, targets/benchmarks, colour coding (green/yellow/red against targets), and brief annotations.

steps.title: Create an insights summary
steps.description: Add a section at the top: '3 Key Insights This Month' — written in plain English. What's working, what's not, and what action should be taken.
steps.tips: This is the most important part. Numbers tell you what happened; insights tell you what to do about it.

steps.title: Design for readability
steps.description: Clean layout, consistent formatting, logical flow from high-level to detail. Make it something someone can scan in 30 seconds and understand the headline story.

deliverables: Live dashboard (Looker Studio or Google Sheets)

rubric.description: Metrics are relevant, covering the full funnel, and aligned with stated business goals.
Numbers are correctly sourced and calculations are verified.
Dashboard is clean, scannable, and uses appropriate chart types.
The insights summary translates data into clear next steps.
